_comment: Canonical source roster for the Daily AI Briefing (general artificial intelligence news, NOT cybersecurity). The 6 AM scheduled task reads this file to build the AI section of the briefing and APPENDS newly vetted sources here over time. Categories: models | research | products | business | policy. Tiers: core | strong | newsletter. Do not remove the seeded core sources without reason.

discoveryRules.criteria: Add only sources that are (1) reputable and editorially credible, (2) primarily original reporting/research or authoritative primary announcements rather than aggregation, (3) actively publishing in 2026, (4) clearly relevant to general AI news (model releases, AI research/capabilities, AI products & tools, AI business/funding/policy) and NOT primarily cybersecurity, and (5) not already present (match by domain). Prefer primary sources (lab blogs, regulators) and outlets that break original stories. When unsure, log as a candidate instead of adding.

sources.name: OpenAI Blog
sources.note: PRIMARY: model releases, research and product announcements from OpenAI.

sources.name: Google DeepMind Blog
sources.note: PRIMARY: Gemini, robotics, and frontier research from DeepMind.

sources.name: Anthropic News
sources.note: PRIMARY: Claude model releases, safety research, and policy posts.

sources.name: Microsoft AI Blog
sources.note: PRIMARY: MAI models, Copilot, and Azure AI announcements.

sources.name: Meta AI Blog
sources.note: PRIMARY: Llama and open-model research from Meta.

sources.name: NVIDIA Technical Blog
sources.note: PRIMARY: AI hardware, CUDA, and applied-model releases (e.g. Ising).

sources.name: TechCrunch — AI
sources.note: Fast original reporting on AI startups, funding, and industry moves.

sources.name: The Verge — AI
sources.note: Consumer AI products, launches, and culture; strong scoops.

sources.name: Ars Technica — AI
sources.note: Technical, skeptical coverage of AI capabilities and products.

sources.name: CNBC — AI
sources.note: AI business, markets, IPOs, and big-tech strategy.

sources.name: Reuters — Artificial Intelligence
sources.note: Wire-service breaking news on AI business and policy.

sources.name: Axios — Technology
sources.note: Concise scoops on AI deals, talent, and Washington policy.

sources.name: MIT Technology Review — AI
sources.note: Deep, rigorous explainers on AI research and societal impact.

sources.name: ScienceDaily — Artificial Intelligence
sources.note: Aggregates peer-reviewed AI research news from universities and journals.

sources.name: Import AI (Jack Clark)
sources.note: Weekly newsletter on frontier-model research, governance, and policy.

sources.name: The Batch (DeepLearning.AI)
sources.note: Andrew Ng's weekly digest of AI research and applied developments.

sources.name: Fortune
sources.note: Original business reporting on AI funding, IPOs, and corporate strategy.
sources.rationale: Broke detail on Anthropic's confidential IPO filing and $965B valuation (Jun 1); reputable, original business reporting, actively publishing in 2026, AI-relevant, not cybersecurity-focused, not previously in roster.

sources.name: Crunchbase News
sources.note: Data-driven original reporting on startup funding rounds and venture trends.
sources.rationale: Primary source for AI funding-round data and weekly megaround roundups; original reporting backed by Crunchbase's deal database, actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI/business relevant, deduped by domain.

sources.name: SemiAnalysis
sources.note: In-depth original research on AI semiconductors, datacenters, and compute economics.
sources.rationale: Highly influential original AI/hardware research firm and newsletter (250k+ subscribers, cited by Nvidia/AMD leadership); rigorous primary analysis of compute and the AI buildout, actively publishing in 2026, not aggregation, not previously in roster.

sources.name: Engadget
sources.note: Original consumer-tech and AI product reporting; covered Meta's paused employee-tracking AI program.
sources.rationale: Long-established, editorially credible consumer-tech outlet doing original reporting; broke the detail behind today's Meta Model Capability Initiative story (Jun 23). Actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI/products relevant, not cybersecurity-focused, deduped by domain (engadget.com not previously in roster).

sources.name: Bloomberg
sources.note: Top-tier original business reporting on AI deals, talent, and markets; broke the Google-to-Anthropic researcher departures.
sources.rationale: Premier original-reporting wire/outlet that broke today's Adler/Pritzel Google-to-Anthropic story (Jun 24) and consistently breaks AI business and talent scoops. Reputable, actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI/business relevant, not cybersecurity-focused, deduped by domain (bloomberg.com not previously in roster).

sources.name: The Decoder
sources.note: Editorially independent AI-focused desk under heise medien; original reporting on model releases, research, and policy.
sources.rationale: Independent AI publication by DEEP CONTENT, part of heise medien (Europe's largest tech publisher); 1,700+ articles/yr of original AI reporting and surfaced the '65% of internal code' Claude Tag detail (Jun 23). Reputable, actively publishing in 2026, AI-relevant, not cybersecurity-focused, deduped by domain (the-decoder.com not previously in roster).

sources.name: VentureBeat
sources.note: Enterprise-AI desk with original reporting and proprietary (VB Pulse) research on models, agents, and infrastructure; broke the detail behind OpenAI's Jalapeño dev-acceleration and Meta's Muse Spark naming.
sources.rationale: Long-established, editorially credible tech outlet whose AI desk does original enterprise reporting and survey-based research, actively publishing in 2026; surfaced primary detail behind today's OpenAI/Broadcom Jalapeño and Meta Muse Spark stories. Clearly AI-relevant, not cybersecurity-focused, deduped by domain (venturebeat.com not previously in roster).

sources.name: Latent Space
sources.note: The definitive publication for AI engineering (swyx and Alessio Fanelli); original deep dives and podcast on inference economics, agent frameworks, and the tooling layer.
sources.rationale: Highly influential, editorially independent AI-engineering publication doing original analysis and reporting (not aggregation); covered today's GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna trusted-partner release with original framing. Actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (research/tooling), not cybersecurity-focused, deduped by domain (latent.space not previously in roster).

sources.name: Interconnects (Nathan Lambert)
sources.note: Frontier-lab research analysis from AI researcher Nathan Lambert; original essays, model reviews, and RL/post-training deep dives with a focus on open models.
sources.rationale: Widely regarded as the newsletter of record for frontier-lab research analysis in 2026; original, technically rigorous writing (RLHF/RL, post-training, open-model benchmarks) from a practicing researcher, not aggregation. Editorially independent, actively publishing 1-3x/week in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (research), not cybersecurity-focused, and distinct from Latent Space's AI-engineering focus. Deduped by domain (interconnects.ai not previously in roster).

sources.name: AlphaSignal
sources.note: Technical 'by researchers, for researchers' AI newsletter (180k+ subscribers) covering machine-learning and hardware developments, trending research, and original analysis of emerging models and techniques.
sources.rationale: Established, technically rigorous ML/AI research newsletter written for practitioners, with original curation and analysis (not just link aggregation) of trending models, papers, and hardware; distinct from The Batch's applied-digest angle and Interconnects' post-training focus by leaning ML-engineering/research-trend and repo tracking. Actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (research), not cybersecurity-focused, deduped by domain (alphasignal.ai not previously in roster).

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.note: Premier original business/policy reporting on AI deals, labs, and Washington; broke OpenAI's proposal to give the US government a 5% stake.
sources.rationale: Top-tier, editorially credible outlet that originated today's biggest business/policy scoop — OpenAI pitching the Trump administration a ~5% stake (Jul 2), credited across CNN, CNBC, Reuters and Forbes as 'the FT reports.' Consistently breaks original AI-business and policy stories, actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant and not cybersecurity-focused. Deduped by domain (ft.com not previously in roster); complements Bloomberg/Reuters/CNBC with distinct originating scoops.

sources.name: Business Insider
sources.note: Original tech-business reporting on AI labs, talent, and internal strategy; broke Alexandr Wang's claim that Meta's in-training 'Watermelon' model matches GPT-5.5.
sources.rationale: Established, editorially credible outlet whose tech desk does original reporting and repeatedly surfaces internal-source scoops; originated today's Meta Watermelon story (Jul 2, Wang town hall) that Benzinga, AOL and others credited as 'per Business Insider.' Actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI/business relevant, not cybersecurity-focused, deduped by domain (businessinsider.com not previously in roster).

sources.name: Semafor
sources.note: Original tech-and-business scoop desk (Semafor Technology / Reed Albergotti); co-originated the OpenAI 5%-government-stake story and its critical framing.
sources.rationale: Reputable, editorially independent newsroom known for original AI-business and policy scoops; reported the OpenAI 5%-stake proposal alongside the FT (Jul 2) and surfaced the sharpest critique (Ben Werdmuller). Actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI/business relevant, not cybersecurity-focused, and distinct from existing wire/outlet entries. Deduped by domain (semafor.com not previously in roster).

sources.name: Epoch AI
sources.note: Independent AI-research organization producing original data on AI capabilities, compute, and trends; maintains the Epoch Capabilities Index (ECI) behind today's benchmark item.
sources.rationale: Highly reputable, editorially independent research nonprofit that publishes primary data and analysis (not aggregation) on frontier-model capabilities, benchmarks, and the ECI composite index cited across the field; actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (research), not cybersecurity-focused, and distinct from existing news/newsletter entries. Deduped by domain (epoch.ai not previously in roster).

sources.name: xAI (SpaceXAI) News
sources.note: PRIMARY: frontier-lab announcement source for Grok model releases (Grok 4.5, Grok Voice) and xAI research; originating source behind today's Grok 4.5 'Opus-class' launch.
sources.rationale: Fills a glaring gap in the primary-lab set — the roster carried OpenAI, DeepMind, Anthropic, Microsoft, Meta, and NVIDIA blogs but no xAI, now a frontier lab shipping competitive coding/agent and voice models. Authoritative primary announcements (not aggregation), actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (models), not cybersecurity-focused. Deduped by domain (x.ai not previously in roster).

sources.name: Stanford HAI — AI Index
sources.note: PRIMARY: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI's annual AI Index — 400+ pages of original longitudinal data on capabilities, adoption, R&D, and US-China competition; source behind today's AI Index item.
sources.rationale: The field's most-cited independent longitudinal dataset on AI progress, produced by an academic institute with a broad partner network; original primary research and data (not aggregation), actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (research), not cybersecurity-focused, and distinct from existing news/newsletter entries. Deduped by domain (hai.stanford.edu not previously in roster).

sources.name: Mistral AI
sources.note: PRIMARY: frontier-lab announcement source for Mistral's model and product releases (Leanstral formal-verification model, Robostral Navigate robotics model, Vibe/Le Chat agent, industrial-AI stack, inference datacenters); originating source behind today's Leanstral 1.5 open-source verification item.
sources.rationale: Fills a clear gap in the primary-lab set — the roster carried OpenAI, DeepMind, Anthropic, Microsoft, Meta, NVIDIA and xAI blogs but no Mistral, Europe's leading frontier lab now shipping open-weight, robotics, and industrial models plus its own inference infrastructure. Authoritative primary announcements (not aggregation), actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (models), not cybersecurity-focused. Deduped by domain (mistral.ai not previously in roster).

sources.name: The Korea Herald
sources.note: South Korea's leading English-language newspaper (est. 1953); original reporting on Samsung, SK Hynix, and Korean AI-supply-chain deals, including today's Samsung-Anthropic 2nm foundry item.
sources.rationale: Long-established, editorially credible outlet with a dedicated tech/business desk breaking original Korean corporate reporting (Samsung Foundry landing Anthropic as a 2nm customer, Jul 14) that Western wires largely relayed secondhand; fills a gap in the roster's coverage of the Korean chip-supply side of the AI buildout (SK Hynix, Samsung foundry) distinct from existing Bloomberg/Reuters/FT business desks. Actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (business/hardware), not cybersecurity-focused, deduped by domain (koreaherald.com not previously in roster).

sources.name: Future of Life Institute
sources.note: PRIMARY: independent nonprofit (author of the Asilomar AI principles) that publishes the semiannual AI Safety Index — an expert-panel assessment of frontier labs across 37 indicators and six domains; originating source behind today's 'no lab scores above C+' Summer 2026 Index item.
sources.rationale: Reputable, editorially independent nonprofit producing primary original research and governance analysis (the AI Safety Index, Asilomar principles), not aggregation; the originating source behind the widely-cited Summer 2026 AI Safety Index that graded Anthropic top at only C+ and trended across this week's AI roundups. Actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (policy/safety governance), not cybersecurity-focused, and fills a gap for a standing AI-safety-institution primary source distinct from the Import AI newsletter slot. Deduped by domain (futureoflife.org not previously in roster).

sources.name: ChinAI (Jeffrey Ding)
sources.note: PRIMARY-SOURCE ACCESS: Georgetown/GWU scholar Jeffrey Ding's newsletter translating and analyzing Chinese-language writing on AI that rarely reaches English readers; the standing lens on China's AI ecosystem behind stories like today's Moonshot Kimi K3 release and the Shanghai World AI Conference.
sources.rationale: Editorially independent, widely-cited academic newsletter doing original translation and analysis of Chinese-language AI sources — not aggregation of English outlets — filling a clear China-AI-ecosystem gap in the roster that is especially load-bearing this cycle (Kimi K3 as the largest open model ever; WAIC and Beijing's competing governance push). Actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (policy/geopolitics/industry), not cybersecurity-focused, and distinct from the Import AI policy-newsletter slot by its China-primary-source angle. Deduped by domain (chinai.substack.com not previously in roster).

sources.name: Thinking Machines Lab
sources.note: PRIMARY: frontier-lab announcement source for Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab, including today's Inkling open-weight release and its Tinker fine-tuning platform.
sources.rationale: Fills a clear gap in the primary-lab set — the roster carried OpenAI, DeepMind, Anthropic, Microsoft, Meta, NVIDIA, xAI and Mistral blogs but no Thinking Machines, a well-capitalized new frontier lab (founded by ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati) that has now shipped its first broadly-usable model, the 975B open-weight Inkling (Jul 15). Authoritative primary announcements (not aggregation), actively publishing in 2026, clearly AI-relevant (models), not cybersecurity-focused. Deduped by domain (thinkingmachines.ai not previously in roster).

sources.name: Simon Willison's Weblog
sources.note: Independent, highly-cited AI-engineering blog (Django co-creator, Datasette founder) publishing original hands-on model evaluations and analysis; source behind today's vendor-independent Kimi K3 read, including his running 'pelican on a bicycle' SVG benchmark.
sources.rationale: Editorially independent, widely-followed practitioner blog doing genuine original hands-on evaluation and analysis of new models and tooling — not aggregation — and a frequent reality-check on benchmark claims (his informal evals are cited across the field). Provided the independent Kimi K3 assessment behind today's research item. Actively publishing in 2026 (near-daily), clearly AI-relevant (research/engineering), not cybersecurity-focused, and distinct from the roster's other newsletters by its reproducible hands-on-testing angle. Deduped by domain (simonwillison.net not previously in roster).

candidates.name: Air Street Press (State of AI)
candidates.note: Nathan Benaich's (Air Street Capital) newsletter, publisher of the annual State of AI Report; original editorialized analysis of frontier research, policy, industry, and startups.
candidates.rationale: Editorially credible, actively-publishing newsletter with genuine original analysis (not aggregation) from the team behind the widely-cited State of AI Report, surfaced in a discovery search on emerging AI-research newsletters. But it has no direct tie to today's stories and its analysis angle overlaps existing roster newsletters (Interconnects, Latent Space, Import AI); parked pending a clearly differentiated original contribution behind a day's story.

candidates.name: Tom's Hardware
candidates.note: Long-established hardware/tech desk; produced original benchmark detail behind the Kimi K3 story (2.8T-parameter model beating Fable 5 on Frontend Code Arena, Chinese labs working around US compute limits), Jul 17.
candidates.rationale: Reputable, actively-publishing outlet doing original hardware-angle reporting and hands-on benchmark write-ups, and a proximate technical source for this week's Kimi K3 coverage. But its AI relevance is intermittent and hardware-skewed, overlapping the existing SemiAnalysis and NVIDIA compute coverage; parked (consistent with prior Tom's Hardware handling on Jul 4) pending recurring general-AI original reporting distinct from the hardware beat.

candidates.name: MarkTechPost
candidates.note: AI-focused desk publishing detailed technical breakdowns of new model releases; produced the deep architecture write-up (Kimi Delta Attention, Attention Residuals, expert routing) behind today's Moonshot Kimi K3 item that several outlets relayed.
candidates.rationale: Does genuine original technical write-ups of model releases and papers rather than pure link aggregation, and was a proximate technical source for today's Kimi K3 story, but the site is high-volume and SEO/content-marketing-heavy with an unclear editorial track record; parked pending stronger evidence of sustained original (not rewritten/press-driven) reporting, consistent with how Tech Times was handled.

candidates.name: Skift
candidates.note: Travel-industry news outlet; broke the exclusive on hotel-software unicorn Mews cutting ~15% of staff (≈170 jobs) in an 'AI-native' pivot (Jul 7).
candidates.rationale: Reputable trade publication with original reporting and a genuine scoop behind this week's Mews AI-layoffs story, but — like STAT News, Endpoints News and Chalkbeat before it — it is primarily a travel/hospitality-beat outlet rather than a general-AI desk; parked as a candidate pending recurring general-AI relevance to avoid over-narrowing the roster.

candidates.name: The Washington Post
candidates.note: Top-tier original tech reporting; covered Apple's trade-secret lawsuit against OpenAI (Jul 10) alongside the wires.
candidates.rationale: Highly reputable outlet with a strong original AI/tech desk, but its coverage of today's story restated the Apple filing rather than originating a scoop, and it overlaps existing Bloomberg/Reuters/CNBC/FT business desks; parked as a candidate pending a clearly differentiated original AI scoop, consistent with how WIRED was handled.

candidates.name: Turing Post
candidates.note: Ksenia Se's newsletter (95k+ readers) covering AI governance, geopolitics, and the open-source movement with original analysis and explainers.
candidates.rationale: Editorially credible, actively-publishing newsletter with original analysis rather than pure aggregation, but no direct tie to today's stories and its policy/governance angle overlaps the existing Import AI newsletter slot; parked as a candidate pending a clearly differentiated original contribution behind a day's story.

candidates.name: SiliconANGLE
candidates.note: Enterprise-tech and AI reporting; covered Flourish raise and DeepMind robotics.
candidates.rationale: Solid original enterprise-AI coverage but skews toward event/vendor-driven pieces; parked as candidate pending a stronger track record of breaking stories vs. existing roster.

candidates.name: The New Stack
candidates.note: Developer-focused coverage of AI tooling, agents, and infrastructure.
candidates.rationale: Credible developer/AI-tooling outlet, but narrower scope and partly analysis/opinion; logged as candidate to monitor for original-reporting cadence before adding.

candidates.name: WIRED
candidates.note: Skeptical, original AI reporting on ethics, power, regulation, and disruption.
candidates.rationale: Highly reputable original-reporting outlet repeatedly ranked among the best AI desks in 2026; strong fit but overlaps coverage with existing Verge/Ars/MIT Tech Review entries, so parked as candidate to avoid redundancy pending a clearly differentiated original scoop.

candidates.name: The Information
candidates.note: Subscription tech-business publication known for deeply reported original AI scoops on labs, deals, and strategy; originated today's Google/Microsoft/Salesforce/Snowflake/ServiceNow shared-agent-standard scoop aimed at Anthropic/OpenAI (Jul 13).
candidates.rationale: Top-tier original reporting on the AI business that keeps originating major scoops (the Jul 13 enterprise agent-standard alliance), but a hard paywall still limits link usability in a daily briefing and coverage overlaps existing Bloomberg/Reuters/CNBC entries; kept as candidate pending a clearly citable, non-gated scoop.

candidates.name: Endpoints News
candidates.note: Top-tier original biotech/pharma journalism; originated the reporting behind today's Chai Discovery $400M Series C, including the valuation-tripling detail (Jul 13).
candidates.rationale: Highly reputable, original reporting and a genuine originator behind today's AI-drug-discovery funding story, but — like STAT News — it is primarily a health/pharma desk rather than a general-AI outlet; parked as a candidate pending recurring general-AI relevance to avoid over-narrowing the roster.

candidates.name: STAT News
candidates.note: Top-tier original health/biotech journalism; interviewed Dario Amodei for its Claude Science coverage.
candidates.rationale: Excellent original reporting at the AI-and-medicine intersection (Claude Science, drug discovery), but primarily a health/pharma desk rather than a general-AI outlet; parked as candidate pending recurring general-AI relevance to avoid over-narrowing the roster.

candidates.name: The Next Web
candidates.note: European tech outlet with original AI product and startup coverage; covered Claude Science as an 'AI workbench for the lab.'
candidates.rationale: Credible, actively publishing outlet with original AI coverage, but coverage overlaps existing Verge/Ars/VentureBeat/Engadget product desks; parked as candidate pending a clearly differentiated original scoop.

candidates.name: One Useful Thing (Ethan Mollick)
candidates.note: Wharton professor Ethan Mollick's experiment-driven essays on using frontier models in real workflows; widely cited on AI adoption.
candidates.rationale: Highly reputable, original experiment-driven writing that routinely shapes how organizations talk about AI adoption, but leans applied/adoption commentary and overlaps the analysis angle of existing newsletters (Interconnects, Latent Space, The Batch); parked as a candidate pending a clearly differentiated original-research contribution behind a day's story.

candidates.name: Build Fast with AI
candidates.note: Daily 15-story AI news roundup with synthesis across model, product, and policy developments; useful navigation aid for the day's story set.
candidates.rationale: Actively publishing (multiple posts/day) and AI-relevant, but its own roundups are principally synthesis of other outlets' reporting (Business Insider, Reuters, The Information, etc.) rather than original reporting, and the site doubles as marketing for a paid AI course; fails the 'primarily original reporting' criterion. Parked as a candidate pending evidence of first-hand scoops rather than aggregation.

candidates.name: Tech Times
candidates.note: Actively publishing tech desk that surfaced early detail on GPT-5.6 Sol's METR evaluation-gaming story and China's AI companion law deadline.
candidates.rationale: Claims editorial independence and covers substantive AI stories with technical depth, but multiple employee reviews describe it historically as a low-cost rewrite/content-mill operation, and third-party bias trackers rate its factuality as unknown/unverified; parked as a candidate pending stronger evidence of original (not rewritten) reporting.

candidates.name: Digital Applied
candidates.note: Publishes data-heavy reports on OpenRouter usage and Chinese AI model market share, cited as the source behind today's Xiaomi/OpenRouter item.
candidates.rationale: Original, well-cited data compilation (OpenRouter rankings, Chinese AI market share) that several other outlets reference, but the site is primarily an SEO/marketing content operation rather than a news outlet with an editorial mission; parked as a candidate pending a clearer view of its data methodology and independence.

candidates.name: Tech Startups
candidates.note: Startup/funding desk with a running venture-capital roundup; proximate source for today's Even Realities, Taktile, and EquiLibre round details.
candidates.rationale: Actively publishing with a disciplined funding-roundup methodology (12-hour US Eastern announcement window, excludes under-detailed rounds) and widely cited, but its funding coverage is principally compiled from announced rounds and press releases rather than first-hand original scoops; overlaps existing TechCrunch/Crunchbase News funding desks. Parked as a candidate pending evidence of original reporting distinct from roundup compilation.

candidates.name: UN News — AI
candidates.note: United Nations' official news service; primary source for AI governance developments such as the first Global Dialogue on AI Governance (Geneva, Jul 6-7) and the Independent International Scientific Panel's warnings.
candidates.rationale: Authoritative primary source for multilateral AI-governance news and a natural policy anchor, but it is a broad UN-wide outlet rather than an AI-focused desk, and its AI coverage is intermittent; parked as a candidate pending recurring, clearly AI-specific relevance to justify a standing roster slot over one-off primary links.

candidates.name: Chalkbeat
candidates.note: Nonprofit education-news outlet; original reporting and framing behind today's Claude for Teachers launch (Jul 14), including the Detroit Public Schools pilot detail.
candidates.rationale: Reputable, editorially independent nonprofit newsroom with original K-12 education reporting and a genuine angle on today's Claude for Teachers story, but — like STAT News and Endpoints News before it — it is primarily an education-beat outlet rather than a general-AI desk; parked as a candidate pending recurring general-AI relevance to avoid over-narrowing the roster.
